name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description: GenAI Client is a Java library for provider-neutral integration with generative AI services in Machai applications. It centralizes provider resolution, runtime configuration, prompt and instruction handling, file and URL context, optional host-side function tools, embedding requests, and token-usage reporting. The library supports OpenAI-compatible providers, Anthropic Claude, EPAM CodeMie delegation, structured Java tool registration, web-search and MCP-related provider capabilities, semantic-search embeddings, automated content generation, and intelligent project assembly workflows. Applications typically resolve a concrete Genai provider from a model identifier such as OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini, Claude:claude-3-5-sonnet, CodeMie-hosted model identifiers, or a fully qualified provider class name; initialize the provider with Configurator settings; add prompts, instructions, files, URLs, or service-loaded tools; execute generation or embedding operations; and aggregate Usage metrics for monitoring.

usageContext: Java applications that need a common Genai interface for multiple LLM providers
Machai modules that resolve providers dynamically from model identifiers
Request-scoped AI workflows that combine instructions, prompts, files, URLs, and tools
Applications exposing host Java methods as AI-callable function tools through ServiceLoader
Semantic search and similarity workflows that require embedding vectors
Monitoring workflows that aggregate input, cached-input, and output token usage by model

constructors.description: Creates and initializes a request-scoped Genai provider by resolving a provider identifier such as OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini or a fully qualified provider class name. The manager applies the selected model to the runtime Configurator and returns a concrete provider implementation for prompt execution, tool use, embedding support when available, and usage inspection.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.macha.core.commons.config.Configurator configurator = ...;
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai provider = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider("OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini", configurator);
examples.description: Resolves an OpenAI-backed provider from a Provider:Model identifier and initializes it with application configuration before use. The configuration should include provider credentials such as OPENAI_API_KEY and any optional endpoint, timeout, output-token, or logging settings.

constructors.description: Constructs an OpenAI-backed Genai implementation. After construction, call init with a model name and Configurator so the provider can create OpenAI-compatible clients, prepare response and embedding requests, register tools, enable optional web search or MCP integrations, and record usage metrics.

examples.description: Creates, initializes, configures, and invokes an OpenAI provider directly. Use this when application code wants to bypass provider-name resolution and explicitly instantiate the OpenAI implementation.

constructors.description: Constructs an Anthropic-backed Genai implementation that adapts Anthropic Claude message APIs to the common Machai provider contract. The provider supports prompt accumulation, function-tool loops, optional web search, MCP server forwarding, and token usage capture after initialization with Claude configuration.

examples.description: Creates and invokes a Claude provider directly. The Configurator should provide ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, chatModel or model settings, and optional ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, GENAI_TIMEOUT, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, MAX_TOOL_CALLS, and cacheThreshold values.

constructors.description: Constructs a CodeMie Genai implementation that authenticates against the EPAM CodeMie token endpoint, obtains OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens, configures the CodeMie Code Assistant API base URL, and delegates supported model families to OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible providers.

examples.description: Creates a CodeMie provider directly. Configure GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD for password-grant authentication or client credentials for service-to-service use; optional AUTH_URL can override the token endpoint.

constructors.description: Obtains the singleton utility that discovers FunctionTools implementations through Java ServiceLoader and applies their annotated Tool and Prompt methods to compatible Genai providers. This constructor-like access point prepares host-provided capabilities for provider registration.

examples.description: Loads service-registered Java tool collections and applies them to a provider before prompt execution. This is useful when a request should allow the model to call controlled host functions.

features.name: Resolve generative AI providers dynamically
features.description: Loads and initializes a Genai implementation from a model identifier. Simple provider names are mapped by convention to provider packages, while provider names containing package separators are treated as fully qualified class names. This enables application code to select OpenAI, Claude, CodeMie, or custom providers at runtime.

examples.description: Typical provider-neutral generation workflow: resolve the provider, add instructions and a prompt, then execute the request with perform.

features.name: Common Genai lifecycle and execution contract
features.description: Defines the shared application-facing API for generative AI providers, including initialization, instructions, prompts, URL or file context, tool registration, execution, embedding-related capabilities where supported, usage inspection, working-directory propagation, and cleanup behavior.

examples.description: Uses only the Genai abstraction, allowing the same calling code to work with different provider implementations and still collect usage data.

features.name: OpenAI response generation, tool calls, web search, MCP, files, and embeddings
features.description: Adapts the common Genai API to OpenAI-compatible services. It builds response and embedding requests from instructions, prompts, file references, and registered Java tools; can run iterative tool-call cycles until final text is available; supports optional built-in web search and MCP server integrations; and converts OpenAI usage metrics to Machai usage records.

examples.description: Registers a host-side function tool with an OpenAI provider, asks the model to use available tools, and receives the final response after the tool-call loop completes.

features.name: Anthropic Claude message execution and tool-use loop
features.description: Adapts Anthropic Java SDK message APIs to Machai's Genai interface. It builds Claude message requests from prompts and instructions, registers custom function tools, handles tool-use response blocks by invoking matching ToolFunction callbacks and resubmitting requests, supports optional web search and MCP server forwarding, and captures usage statistics.

examples.description: Configures Claude with a custom function tool. If Claude requests the tool, the provider invokes the callback and continues until Claude returns a plain text answer.

features.name: EPAM CodeMie authentication and delegated provider routing
features.description: Integrates with EPAM CodeMie by obtaining OAuth 2.0 access tokens through password or client-credentials authentication, configuring CodeMie-hosted endpoints, and delegating model requests to OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible provider implementations. OpenAI-compatible, Gemini-compatible, and embedding models are routed to the OpenAI implementation; Claude-compatible models are routed to the Anthropic implementation.

examples.description: Uses CodeMie through the manager. Runtime configuration supplies CodeMie credentials and endpoint settings; application code continues using the common Genai interface.

features.name: ServiceLoader-based function tools and prompt metadata
features.description: Discovers FunctionTools implementations with Java ServiceLoader and registers annotated Tool and Prompt methods with compatible providers. Param, ParamDescriptor, Role, SupportedFor, ToolFunction, and SpecialException provide metadata and execution semantics for describing parameters, reusable prompts, supported applications, callback invocation, and controlled task-flow signaling.

examples.description: Defines a Java tool collection and applies discovered tools to a provider. Register the implementation through Java ServiceLoader metadata so FunctionToolsLoader can discover it at runtime.

features.name: Token usage records and aggregated statistics
features.description: Provides a central registry for aggregated GenAI token-usage statistics. Individual provider invocations expose immutable Usage records, and usage entries can be grouped by model identifier for logging and inspection of input, cached input, and output token consumption.

examples.description: Records and logs usage for a model after a request. This helps applications monitor total token consumption across multiple request-scoped provider instances.

features.name: Structured invocation of registered application tools
features.description: Provides a tools-oriented provider implementation that collects prompts, registers ToolFunction instances, and invokes host-defined functions from structured YAML request data containing a tool name and params object. It is intended for internal provider orchestration when tool functions need the same lifecycle as other AI providers.

examples.description: Invokes a registered tool using a YAML-style request with a tool entry and params map.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
customizations.description: Implement this service-provider interface to install host-provided function tools and prompt-producing methods into compatible Genai providers. Implementations are discovered by FunctionToolsLoader through Java ServiceLoader and may be limited to specific application classes with SupportedFor.

examples.description: Creates a service-loaded tool collection whose annotated methods can be registered as AI-accessible function tools.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction
customizations.description: Implement or provide this functional interface when registering a callback directly with a provider. The callback receives structured JSON parameters, the provider working directory, and configuration, allowing controlled host logic to be executed during model tool calls.

examples.snippet: provider.addTool("get_status", "Returns status", (params, projectDir) -> "ready", new org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ParamDescriptor("project", "string", "Project name", true));
examples.description: Registers an inline ToolFunction callback and parameter descriptor on a provider.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.GenaiAdapter
customizations.description: Use the delegating Genai implementation as a wrapper, adapter, or decorator around another Genai instance to add cross-cutting behavior while preserving the common provider contract.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.AbstractAIProvider
customizations.description: Extend this base class when implementing a new provider integration that should reuse common configuration-driven behavior, timeout handling, request logging, optional web-search support, MCP server registration, and guarded tool invocation.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai
studs.description: Primary interface for generative AI provider integrations. Implement this interface to expose a new model provider through the common Machai lifecycle for initialization, instructions, prompts, tool registration, execution, usage inspection, and cleanup.

examples.description: Uses the Genai interface to keep application code provider-neutral.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider
studs.description: Contract for AI embedding providers. Implement this interface when a provider can generate embedding vectors for semantic search, similarity, and retrieval workflows.

examples.description: Checks whether a resolved provider supports embeddings before using embedding-specific operations.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: Service-provider interface for installing host-provided function tools and prompt metadata. Implementations are discovered through Java ServiceLoader and applied to providers by FunctionToolsLoader.

examples.description: Defines reusable prompt metadata that can be discovered and registered by the loader.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction
studs.description: Functional interface representing a tool callable by a provider during a run. It is the direct execution contract used when providers invoke registered host functions.

examples.title: Install GenAI Client as a Maven dependency
examples.description: Add the library to a Maven application that wants provider-neutral generative AI integration. Because this artifact is distributed as a Java library, installation is normally performed by adding its Maven coordinates and ensuring provider credentials are available at runtime.
steps: Add a dependency on org.machanism.machai:genai-client:1.2.0-SNAPSHOT to the application pom.xml.
Provide required provider configuration such as OPENAI_API_KEY for OpenAI, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for Claude, or GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD for CodeMie.
Create or obtain an org.machanism.macha.core.commons.config.Configurator instance with the provider settings.
Resolve a provider with org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager and invoke the common Genai API.
examples.snippet:
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.machanism.machai</groupId>
  <artifactId>genai-client</artifactId>
  <version>1.2.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>
examples.configuration: OPENAI_API_KEY must be configured for OpenAI-backed requests. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be configured for Anthropic-backed requests. CodeMie requests require CodeMie credentials such as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD or client credentials, with AUTH_URL optional when overriding the token endpoint.
examples.run: Compile and run the host Java application normally; GenAI Client is invoked from application code rather than from a standalone CLI.

examples.title: Generate text with a provider resolved by model identifier
examples.description: Shows the standard request-scoped workflow: resolve a provider, initialize it from configuration, add instructions and prompt content, execute the model request, and record usage metrics.
examples.notes: Provider instances are mutable and should generally be treated as request-scoped objects rather than shared singletons.

examples.title: Apply service-loaded Java tools before running a prompt
examples.description: Demonstrates how to configure and run host-side function tools. Tools are implemented as FunctionTools classes with Tool, Prompt, and Param metadata, registered through Java ServiceLoader, and applied to a provider only for requests that need them.
examples.configuration: Register FunctionTools implementations with Java ServiceLoader and restrict them with SupportedFor when multiple applications share the same runtime. Production deployments should expose only deliberate, safe, deterministic host operations.

examples.title: Use Anthropic Claude with a custom tool
examples.description: Shows direct use of the Anthropic provider when an application explicitly selects Claude and wants to register a function tool callback. The provider handles Claude tool-use loops automatically.
examples.configuration: Configure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and optional ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, GENAI_TIMEOUT, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, MAX_TOOL_CALLS, and cacheThreshold values.

examples.title: Route requests through EPAM CodeMie
examples.description: Shows how to use CodeMie as the authenticated provider gateway. CodeMie obtains OAuth 2.0 tokens and delegates supported model families to OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-compatible providers while application code keeps using the common Genai interface.
examples.configuration: Configure GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD for password authentication, or client credentials for service-to-service authentication. AUTH_URL may override the token endpoint when required.
